topics: Study of basic digital ICs and verification of Boolean theorems using digital logic gates
Familiarisation of the working of circuit simulation software: realization of basic logic gates and Boolean functions
Familiarisation of Verilog HDL: modelling of basic gates using gate level, behavioural, structural, and dataflow modelling
Realization of SOP and POS expressions using NAND gates alone and NOR gates alone
Modelling a Boolean function (SOP and POS) in Verilog using continuous assignment with logical operators, conditional operators, and gate level primitives

Design and implementation of combinational logic circuits for arbitrary functions: code converters, half and full adders, half and full subtractors, multiplexers, demultiplexers, encoders, and decoders
Design and implementation of combinational circuits using MSI devices: 4-bit adder and subtractor, parity generator and checker, magnitude comparator, and Boolean function using MUX
Study of D flip flop and JK flip flops using ICs
Design and implementation of shift registers using D flip flops: serial-in serial-out, serial-in parallel-out, parallel-in serial-out, and parallel-in parallel-out
Design and implementation of asynchronous counters: 3-bit up counter, 3-bit down counter, 3-bit up-down counter with mode control, and mod-N counter
Design and implementation of synchronous counters: 3-bit up counter, 3-bit down counter, and sequence generator
Modelling 4:1 MUX, 1:4 DEMUX, 4-to-2 encoder, 2-to-4 decoder, and 7-segment display decoder in Verilog
Design and synthesis of the behavioural model for a D flip flop in Verilog HDL
Design and synthesis of the behavioural model for a synchronous counter in Verilog
Design of a Verilog HDL behavioural model to implement a finite-state machine: serial bit sequence detector
